appDesc.message: See through inflated ratings — removal-adjusted estimates for Google Maps profiles that had reviews removed as “defamation”.
appDesc.description: Extension description (must stay under 132 characters for the Web Store)

badgeCaption.description: Caption in the badge bar (deliberately says 'estimate', not 'rating')

badgeTooltipWorst.message: Google reports that $REMOVED$ review(s) were removed from this profile due to defamation complaints. Worst-case estimate: assumes the top of that range and that every removed review was a 1-star rating. The average could then drop to $CORRECTED$ instead of $OFFICIAL$. Removed reviews aren't necessarily all 1-star — this is an estimate, not a verified rating.

badgeTooltipConservative.message: Google reports that $REMOVED$ review(s) were removed from this profile due to defamation complaints. Conservative estimate: assumes the bottom of that range and that every removed review was a 1-star rating. The average would then be $CORRECTED$ instead of $OFFICIAL$. Removed reviews aren't necessarily all 1-star — this is an estimate, not a verified rating.

checkingText.description: Neutral state while the Reviews tab is probed

cleanCaption.message: No defamation-based review removals reported by Google — respect for letting honest feedback stand.

popupCalcHint.message: Both modes assume every removed review was a 1-star rating — in reality that may not be true. Worst case uses the highest number in Google's reported range, conservative the lowest.

popupWorstCaseHint.message: Upper bound assumed when Google only reports that over 250 reviews were removed.

popupMethodText.message: Google Maps discloses when reviews were removed from a business profile after defamation complaints, but only as a range (e.g. “2 to 5”). ReviewShield recalculates the average as if every removed review had been a 1-star rating, using either the top of the range (worst case) or the bottom (conservative). Removed reviews aren't necessarily all 1-star, so the result is an estimate. Profiles without reported removals get a clean bill instead. Everything runs locally in your browser: no external requests, no analytics, no data collection.
